title: Recovery of Land

statuteRef.label: Time limit for actions to recover land

questions.label: Date adverse possession began
questions.helpText: The date on which the adverse possessor took possession of the land, or the date on which the right of action first accrued to the claimant. Under Schedule 1 of the Limitation Act 1980, this is typically the date of dispossession or discontinuance of possession.

questions.label: Was the claimant under a disability at the date of accrual?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the claimant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained.

questions.label: Was there fraud or deliberate concealment?
questions.helpText: Under s.32 Limitation Act 1980, the limitation period may be postponed if the defendant deliberately concealed facts relevant to the claimant's right of action.

questions.label: Date of discovery
questions.helpText: The date the claimant discovered, or could with reasonable diligence have discovered, the fraud or concealment.

questions.label: Has the adverse possessor acknowledged the claimant's title in writing?
questions.helpText: Under s.29 Limitation Act 1980, a written acknowledgment of the claimant's title by the person in possession causes the limitation period to run afresh.

questions.label: Date of acknowledgment
questions.helpText: The date of the written acknowledgment of title.

notes: For registered land, the Land Registration Act 2002 introduced a separate regime for adverse possession. After 10 years, the squatter can apply for registration; the registered owner then has 2 years to take action. This tool applies the Limitation Act 1980 period only.
The position is significantly different for registered and unregistered land. For registered land post-2003, the LRA 2002 regime generally applies instead of the Limitation Act.
Crown land has different limitation periods. Actions against the Crown may have a 30-year period.
